title: Benoni Defense: Modern
summary: Learn the Benoni, where Black gives up central space for queenside pawn play and piece activity.
description: The Benoni is an aggressive defense where Black allows White a central pawn majority, then attacks it. You'll learn why it's dangerous if White doesn't handle it correctly.
line: 1.d4 Nf6 2.c4 c5 3.d5 e6 4.Nc3 exd5 5.cxd5 d6 6.Nf3 g6 7.Bg5 Bg7 8.e3 O-O 9.Be2
objectives: Understand the Benoni idea: give up space for piece activity.
Master the key: don't let Black's queenside expansion go unchallenged.
plans: White uses the space advantage to attack on the kingside.
Black seeks queenside pressure and central breaks.
The Benoni is double-edged: both sides have dangerous plans.
commonMistakes: Do not attack prematurely. Build up first.
Avoid ignoring Black's queenside. It's dangerous.
Do not underestimate Black's central break.
completionSummary: The Benoni Defense creates sharp, double-edged positions.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.instruction: Play d4 to claim central space.
steps.coaching: White controls the center. Black will respond with the Benoni.
steps.hint: Push the queen pawn to d4.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.coaching: Standard development. The Benoni is coming.

steps.title: Expand in the center
steps.instruction: Play c4 to control more space.
steps.coaching: White claims more central space, inviting the Benoni.

steps.title: The Benoni strike
steps.coaching: This is the Benoni: Black challenges immediately, creating an asymmetrical structure.

steps.title: Accept the pawn structure
steps.instruction: Play d5, accepting the isolated pawn structure.
steps.coaching: White accepts the Benoni structure. Now the real battle begins.

steps.title: Black supports the center
steps.coaching: Black solidifies. The Benoni structure is established.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nc3, developing toward the center.
steps.coaching: Natural development. White controls important squares.
steps.hint: Develop the queen's knight to c3.

steps.title: Black exchanges
steps.instruction: Black takes on d5, opening the game.
steps.coaching: Black exchanges. The position is now open and tactical.

steps.title: White recaptures
steps.coaching: White has the central pawn. Black has active piece play.

steps.title: Black solidifies
steps.coaching: Black prepares development while keeping the position solid.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.coaching: White develops the knight. Castling approaches.

steps.title: Black fianchettoes
steps.coaching: Black prepares the fianchetto for kingside pressure.

steps.title: Black completes fianchetto
steps.coaching: Black's bishop gives strong diagonal pressure.

steps.title: Solidify the center
steps.instruction: Play e3, supporting the d4 pawn.
steps.coaching: White solidifies before castling. The position is complex.

steps.title: Black castles
steps.coaching: Black castles. Both sides are now ready for the battle.

steps.title: Complete development
steps.coaching: White develops the bishop. The real game begins now.
